title: Trioculus
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Otheruses|Oculus}}
{{Citation}}
{{Character
|type=Galactic Empire
|image=[[File:Trioculus2.jpg]]
|name=Trioculus
|homeworld=[[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]<ref name="BT1">{{SW|url=news/barely-tolerable-alien-henchmen-of-the-empire-part-1|series=Barely Tolerable: Alien Henchmen of the Empire|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref>
|birth=
|death=[[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],<ref name="Death date">The events of [[Prophets of the Dark Side (novel)|''Prophets of the Dark Side'']], including Trioculus's death, are dated to [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] in ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> ''[[Moffship]]''<ref name="Prophets of the Dark Side">[[Prophets of the Dark Side (novel)|''Prophets of the Dark Side'']]</ref>
|species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] ([[mutant]])<ref name="BT1" />
|gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]<ref name="BT1" />
|pronouns=He/him<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader">''[[The Glove of Darth Vader]]''</ref>
|height=
|mass=
|hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]] and white<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />
|feathers=

|cyber=

|partners=

|affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="Underworld Appendix">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/89/indexp3.html|text=Underworld Appendix: Swoops, Spice, and Wretched Rogues|int=Underworld Appendix: Swoops, Spice, and Wretched Rogues}}</ref>
*[[Trioculus' Empire]]<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />
|masters=
|apprentices=
}}
{{Quote|Is it so wrong to be a murderer? Or a liar? Or an inhuman monster? I may be all of those things, but I still have a heart.|Trioculus|<ref name="Zorba the Hutt's Revenge">''[[Zorba the Hutt's Revenge]]''</ref>}}
'''Trioculus''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|human]] [[mutant]] who was the [[Supreme Slavelord]] of the [[Spice mines of Kessel/Legends|spice mines]] of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]]. Trioculus had declared himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] a [[5 ABY/Legends|year]] after the apparent [[Battle of Endor/Legends|death]] of [[Darth Sidious|Palpatine]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] and led his own [[Trioculus' Empire|forces]].

The self-proclaimed son of Palpatine, Trioculus was in actuality an impostor that was secretly supported by the [[Central Committee of Grand Moffs]], who wanting to retain their power and prevent [[Ysanne Isard]], former [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]], from taking power and usurping the role of Palpatine's true son, three-eyed mutant [[Triclops]]. Spurred on by a prophecy of [[Rajah Ubooki]], the false [[Kadann]], he sought the [[glove of Darth Vader]] to establish his legitimacy. Trioculus's pursuit of the [[Adventures of a Jedi Prince|Jedi Prince]] [[Ken]] and his infatuation with [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] pitted him against rival [[Zorba Desilijic Tiure]], and led to him being taken captive by the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] and frozen in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]. Trioculus was eventually betrayed by Kadann, who sought the Imperial throne himself, and in the end was [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]] by a [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]].

==Biography==
===Early life===
{{Quote|I dare say neither the slavelord nor the intelligence director is bound for much future greatness.|[[Maximillian Seerdon]]'s evaluation of Trioculus and [[Ysanne Isard]]|<ref name="Underworld Appendix" />}}
Unbeknownst to him, Trioculus was the result of secret [[Scientist/Legends|scientific]] experiments that were conducted at the behest of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], then [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].<ref name="BT1" /> Behind his civilized, almost grandfatherly looks, Palpatine was in fact the public persona of [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Sidious, a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] who was obsessed with piercing the secrets of life and [[Death/Legends|death]].<ref name="DP">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref> Under the watchful eye of [[Sly Moore/Legends|Sly Moore]], Palpatine's advisor,<ref name="BT1" /> the scientists [[Mammon Hoole]] and [[Borborygmus Gog]] experimented into the spontaneous generation of life by utilizing [[Niobi]], a woman from [[Bordal]], as their "test subject."<ref name="AIE">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/96/indexp2.html|series=Aliens in the Empire|text=Part 1: Hierarchy of Power}}</ref> Initial experiments proved very unsuccessful, resulting in deformed, polycephalic [[Homunculus|homunculi]] with misshapen [[Limb/Legends|limbs]]. After constantly putting the matter back on the drawing-board, Hoole and Gog ended up eliminating all abnormalities in their sole successful specimen save one: a third eye, located on the forehead. The resulting [[mutant]] [[Youngling/Legends|child]] was christened "Trioculus."<ref name="BT1" />

What seemed like a disability for the child could in fact have been a blessing. Indeed, the [[Prophets of the Dark Side]] knew of a legend stating that the will of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] would produce a perfectly symmetrical three-eyed being once every millennium. More importantly, the legend added that such a being would be gifted with undeniable power and magnetism. In any event, the birth of Trioculus did not please [[Sarcev Quest]], one of Palpatine's Force-using protégés. The newborn child and his mother Niobi were spirited away on Quest's order,<ref name="AIE" /> and both were deported to the [[Spice mines of Kessel/Legends|spice mines]] of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]], which were managed by the brutal [[Lord Overseer]] [[Reskell Twane]].<ref name="BT1" />

When Trioculus was still very young, his mother met an untimely death in the mines. Perhaps because of his keen intelligence, Twane felt compassion for the three-eyed orphan. He took Trioculus under his protection and even sent him to school.<ref name="BT1" /> The boy's third eye, however, made him a target of ridicule from his schoolmates. Trioculus hated this and became obsessed with exacting vengeance on his tormentors, and over time developed a knack for spying on and reporting his classmates.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

In later years, Trioculus studied military tactics and history and devoted himself to learning the means of manipulation. Trioculus made few friends, but those he had were well placed. He was fully dedicated to the Empire and had extensive knowledge of military matters earned him the friendship of several higher-ups in the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]], including [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] Bertroff Hissa, who oversaw the young man's development, and under his tutelage Trioculus rose to become the ruthless Supreme Slavelord of the [[Spice mines of Kessel/Legends|spice mines]] on his homeworld of Kessel<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" /> by [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]].<ref name="Underworld Appendix" />

Trioculus was infamous for his brutal methods. He worked thousands of slaves to their deaths, had thousands more killed during a [[Kessel revolts|revolt]], and once destroyed a dam to flood out settlers who were causing problems.<ref name="Queen of the Empire">''[[Queen of the Empire]]''</ref> He also allowed [[Glitterstim/Legends|glitterstim]] to be leaked to certain populations to keep them under control, by Palpatine's command. As an agent of the [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]], he informed the organization's leadership of operations on Kessel. However, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Maximillian Seerdon]] of the rival [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] did not see greatness in Trioculus's future.<ref name="Underworld Appendix" />

===Claiming the throne===
{{Quote|We have heard from Trioculus, the Emperor's son, and we shall obey him, one and all!|Bertroff Hissa|<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />}}
In the wake of Palpatine's death at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], many of the surviving remnants of the Empire sought to take control of the Imperial state. Eventually [[Ysanne Isard]], former [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]], rose above the rest. In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]] the [[Central Committee of Grand Moffs]], wanting to retain their power, raised their own candidate as a rival to Isard—Trioculus.<ref name="The New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Declaring him to be the Emperor's son, they rallied thousands of Imperial leaders to join them on Kessel and hear Trioculus's claim to the Imperial throne.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

Trioculus's claim to inheritance of the Imperial throne was strengthened by his resemblance to [[Triclops]], Palpatine's three-eyed son who had once been imprisoned on Kessel.<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi">''[[The Lost City of the Jedi]]''</ref> As Supreme Slavelord, Trioculus had taken a special interest in tormenting Triclops, promising that in time he would usurp his role as Palpatine's son.<ref name="Mission from Mount Yoda">''[[Mission from Mount Yoda]]''</ref> The Grand Moffs had considered Triclops as an Imperial leader, but believing him to be insane decided that Trioculus would be more trustworthy and more willing to serve as a figurehead while the Central Committee held the real power. Though fully aware that it was exercising their own power through his rule, Trioculus consented to this role.<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi" /> For their part, the Moffs showed him great loyalty in return, a fact that did not go unnoticed by the warlord.<ref name="Queen of the Empire" />

[[File:UnlimitedPowahJunior-GODVp22.png|thumb|left|150px|Trioculus blasts a [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] and a [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guard]] who question his right to rule.]]
Though rumors of Palpatine having a son with three eyes had circulated through the Empire, Trioculus was the first to seemingly confirm them. His claim was supported by Grand Moff Hissa, leader of the Central Committee of Grand Moffs who ostensibly served as Trioculus' second-in-command, but in fact was the true power of the Committee. At a secret meeting on Kessel, Trioculus was proclaimed Emperor and named the "new [[Chosen One/Legends|chosen one]]," referring to himself as a "Dark Lord" whose third eye granted him secret powers known to "the ancients."<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

However, his right to rule did not go unquestioned. Many throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] had laid claim to the throne, including [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Josef Grunger]], who had amassed a fleet of thirty [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] and one [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class Star Dreadnought]] at the planet [[Gargon/Legends|Gargon]].<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" /><ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals">{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}}</ref> Additionally, [[Rajah Ubooki]], an impostor of the Prophet of the Dark Side [[Kadann]]<ref name="The Dark Side Sourcebook">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''</ref> had made an equally false "prophecy" that the new Emperor would hold the glove of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], worn on the hand that he had lost in his [[Battle of Endor/Legends#Demise of the Sith|duel]] with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], which was said to have survived the destruction of the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]]. Two present at the convocation, a Grand Admiral<ref>As explained by [[Abel G. Peña]] on the [[Jedi Council Forums]], he intended [[Miltin Takel]] to be this Grand Admiral in the article ''Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals''. However, due to miscommunication with illustrator [[Joe Corroney]], Corroney drew Takel with the appearance of another Imperial seen in a later meeting with Trioculus. As it is, which Grand Admiral was in this scene remains ambiguous. It may be Takel, with some artistic license assumed in the illustration, or it may be an entirely different one; Peña has proposed [[Rufaan Tigellinus]] as a possibility at {{JCFCite|url=posts/26305709|board=Literature|thread=Grand Admirals and Emperor's Pawns articles info|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2007-03-05|time=6:22 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20160405010850/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/grand-admirals-and-emperors-pawns-articles-info.26295788/#post-26305709}}.</ref> and a [[Emperor's Royal Guard/Legends|Royal Guard]], raised this point with Trioculus; in response, he tortured them, seemingly with [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]]. This display quickly earned him the loyalty of those present.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

Though most of the fleet continued to serve Isard,<ref name="The New Essential Chronology" /> Trioculus had the backing of the Grand Moffs, multiple Grand Admirals and many other military leaders and warlords.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" /> He briefly had the allegiance of Grand Admiral [[Miltin Takel]], who had his own reasons for opposing Grunger, but Trioculus had Takel executed when the [[Spice/Legends|spice]]-high admiral expressed doubt at his ability to retain power.<ref name="Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals" />

===The glove of Darth Vader===
{{Quote|Find me the glove of Darth Vader!|Trioculus|<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />}}
[[File:Central_Committee.jpg|thumb|200px|Trioculus commands the Central Committee to find the glove of Darth Vader.]]
To get to a rank were he could get the glove, he posed as the Emperor's son. To quiet any remaining doubts about him, Trioculus determined to find Vader's glove before any other contender for the throne did, and in doing so win the support of the false Kadann. He ordered the Moffs to begin a galaxy-wide search for the glove. He didn't find anything for a long time. As faith in Trioculus began to diminish amongst his supporters, the warlord grew desperate. However, he had a stroke of luck when an Imperial agent, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Dunwell]], discovered several pieces of the Death Star's debris in the [[Valley of the Giant Oysters]] on [[Dac/Legends|Mon Calamari]], one of which had revealed the outline of a glove when scanned.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

Trioculus and Hissa immediately set out for Mon Calamari, where they were greeted by Dunwell at his [[Whaladon Processing Center]], a poaching operation for the native sentient [[Whaladon/Legends|Whaladon]] species. In his [[Whaladon-hunting submarine]], Dunwell took Trioculus and Hissa to the site of the wreckage. Secretly followed by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] in a [[Mon Calamari submersible|Calamarian minisub]], Trioculus retrieved the glove. When Skywalker and Ackbar's minisub was accidentally captured by one of Dunwell's Whaladon-capturing devices, Luke infiltrated the vessel and triggered its self-destruct. Trioculus and Hissa narrowly escaped, the warlord vowing to have his vengeance on Skywalker.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

Despite Trioculus's announcement that he had retrieved the glove, the independent Imperial forces did not immediately fall in line behind him. Grunger and [[Commission for the Preservation of the New Order/Legends|COMPNOR]] declared that they would only give their support with the official blessing of Kadann. Trioculus and Hissa traveled to [[Scardia Station|Space Station Scardia]], the headquarters of the false Prophets, to meet with the Supreme Prophet. Once Kadann forced Trioculus to reveal that he was not, in fact, Palpatine's son, he promptly named him Emperor and gave him his dark blessing.<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi" />

===The Jedi Prince===
{{Quote|Find the Jedi Prince and destroy him—or he will destroy you!|Kadann's prophecy to Trioculus|<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi" />}}
Kadann had another prophecy for Trioculus; the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Ken]], he said, would destroy Trioculus if Trioculus did not destroy him first. He counseled Trioculus to find Ken in the [[Lost City of the Jedi]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]],<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi" /> though he knew the warlord would never succeed.<ref name="Prophets of the Dark Side" />

Trioculus set out in his [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class cruiser]] for Yavin 4. Upon arriving at the moon, he dispatched an [[Imperial anti-security device|explosive-lined probe]] [[Droid/Legends|droid]] to a meeting of the [[Senate Planetary Intelligence Network]], to whom he issued an ultimatum; if they did not provide the location of the Lost City, he would detonate the droid. Luke Skywalker, present at the meeting, quickly disabled the probe and its detonator, forcing Trioculus to resort to his backup plan: burning down the forests of Yavin 4 until he found the city's entrance.<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi" />

[[File:Trioculus_blind.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Trioculus's eyes are examined by MD-5 after he goes blind.]]
Trioculus had devices installed in the glove of Darth Vader to simulate the effects of the Force; they had been taking their toll on his health ever since then, and during the attack Trioculus found himself suddenly struck blind. A [[Ho'Din/Legends|Ho'Din]] named [[Baji]] living on Yavin 4 seemed to be the only solution, and Trioculus had him apprehended. Baji informed him that the rare [[kibo]] plant, found only in the rapidly burning forests of the moon, could cure him. While obtaining it, Trioculus was burned and badly scarred. He eventually decided to remove the true glove and replace it with a replica.<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi" />

In the meantime, the rebels rallied, used the moon's weather control system to quench the fires, and destroyed all but one of Trioculus's ships. The warlord and his coterie retreated. Trioculus again swore vengeance, this time upon the entire [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]—with the exception of [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]].<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi" /> His plan foiled, Trioculus placed a large bounty on Ken.<ref name="Zorba the Hutt's Revenge" />

===Zorba the Hutt===
{{Quote|We met as friends today. We congratulated each other. But from now until the end of time, you and I are sworn enemies! And once a Hutt makes an enemy, there is no retreat until death!|Zorba Desilijic Tiure to Trioculus|<ref name="Zorba the Hutt's Revenge" />}}
[[File:ZorbaTricoulus.jpg|thumb|200px|Trioculus and Zorba the Hutt.]]
In the middle of the crisis, Ysanne Isard arranged for the release of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt's]] father [[Zorba Desilijic Tiure]] from prison to use as an unwitting agent.<ref name="The New Essential Chronology" />

Meanwhile, intent on truly securing the Empire, Trioculus ramped up production of war matériel. One such operation was a massive factory barge located near the core of [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], mining the gas giant's [[Rethin Sea]] to use in the production of weaponry and ammunition. Though the amount of air pollution this facility produced drew complaints from [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] of [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], the warlord was not fazed, threatening an invasion if Calrissian did not capitulate.<ref name="Zorba the Hutt's Revenge" />

While on Bespin visiting [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], Luke Skywalker and his sister Leia Organa accidentally stumbled upon the factory barge. When they approached the huge craft to reconnoiter it, they were shot down, and Leia was taken captive.<ref name="Zorba the Hutt's Revenge" /> From the moment Trioculus saw Leia, he had been smitten with her and wanted her to join him in matrimony.<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi" /> Now that he had her as his prisoner, he set about attempting to win her over.<ref name="Zorba the Hutt's Revenge" />

[[File:Trioculus_carbonite.jpg|thumb|left|130px|Trioculus, frozen in carbonite.]]
Zorba, meanwhile, had taken over Cloud City from Calrissian. Having learned of Leia Organa's complicity in the death of his son, Zorba got wind from an Imperial spy that she was being held by Trioculus. By coincidence, Trioculus's own target, Ken, had been arrested shortly before by Cloud City's police. Seeing an opportunity, Zorba arranged a meeting to negotiate with the warlord. When Trioculus interrogated Ken to assure himself that it was, in fact, the correct boy, he discovered that the danger Ken posed lay in what he had learned from the [[Jedi library]] in the Lost City; Ken was aware that Triclops, and not Trioculus, was the true son of Palpatine.<ref name="Zorba the Hutt's Revenge" />

Trioculus demanded that Zorba give him the boy, and Zorba named his price: that Trioculus remove his factory barge from Bespin and hand over custody of Leia Organa so Zorba could take her life in revenge. The latter was unacceptable to Trioculus, and he ordered his [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to [[Skirmish on Bespin|attack]]. Zorba, however, was prepared, and his greater force of [[Bespin Wing Guard/Legends|Cloud Police]] overwhelmed Trioculus's troops and seized the Imperial leader. Trioculus was transported to the same [[Carbon-freezing chamber/Legends|carbon-freezing chamber]] that Han Solo had once been frozen in. Though he made a last-ditch attempt to bribe Zorba, offering him a share of power and the rank of grand admiral, it was to no avail, and the Hutt had Trioculus encased in a slab of [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]. He placed the warlord's frozen body in the [[Figg & Associates Art Museum]] under heavy guard, keeping him as a hostage to prevent any Imperial retaliation.<ref name="Zorba the Hutt's Revenge" />

===Kadann's coup===
{{Quote|Tormented and frozen alive<br />The three-eyed ruler commands no more.<br />Never again shall he receive<br />The dark blessing of the Supreme Prophet.|Kadann|<ref name="Mission from Mount Yoda" />}}
Trioculus having failed in his mission, the false Kadann now seized the opportunity. From Scardia Station he revoked his blessing of Trioculus, declaring himself the new Emperor. The Central Committee, in the meantime, were working to get Trioculus back. When Zorba left Cloud City for [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], chief of police [[Muskov]] alerted the Imperial forces in orbit of Bespin. Bribing the museum guards, they retrieved Trioculus's body, but before they could thaw it, a vessel of the Prophets arrived, the false High Prophet [[Heingort Giddis|Jedgar]] on board. Jedgar demanded that the moffs relinquish Trioculus; as leverage, he blackmailed Hissa with damaging information the Prophets' agents had collected. Trioculus was handed over and taken to Scardia Station.<ref name="Mission from Mount Yoda" />

When Trioculus's slab arrived at Scardia Station, Kadann and the Prophets had it taken to the station's neutron furnace and exposed it to the furnace's deadly rays. Their heat was such that the block was entirely vaporized, leaving no trace of it or Trioculus. To the Prophets and Moffs, it appeared as though Trioculus were dead.<ref name="Mission from Mount Yoda" />

[[File:ZorbaThrownIntoTheSarlacc-QueenOfTheEmpire.jpg|150px|thumb|Trioculus has Zorba thrown into the [[Sarlacc/Legends|sarlacc]].]]
As it turned out, both parties had been deceived. The block Zorba had displayed in the Cloud City Museum had been a replica. The real body of Trioculus was kept in a hidden compartment on Zorba's ship, the ''[[Zorba Express]]''. It was not until the Grand Moffs' [[Moffship]] intercepted the ''Express'' over Tatooine, intending on avenging themselves upon Zorba for capturing Trioculus, that they found it. Having obtained it, they quickly set about reviving their Emperor. Once free from the carbonite, Trioculus learned that the Prophets had betrayed him, cursed them, then prepared to have his revenge on Zorba. Zorba stalled Trioculus, however, when he informed him that he had Leia imprisoned. Trioculus promised to free him if he gave her location.<ref name="Queen of the Empire" />

When Trioculus found Leia, her feelings towards him had not changed. However, after Trioculus threatened to return her to Zorba, she decided that the warlord was preferable. Taking this as a sign, Trioculus told Hissa to make preparations for a wedding. Then, breaking his vow with Zorba, he had the Hutt thrown into the [[Great Pit of Carkoon/Legends|Great Pit of Carkoon]]. Trioculus had Leia watch, believing this would make her grateful to him and make her accept her own darkness when she saw how much pleasure she took in Zorba's death. This did not seem to be the case, but he nonetheless assigned a detail of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to Leia and left to prepare for the wedding.<ref name="Queen of the Empire" />

===Project Decoy===
For some time, the New Republic had a project in the works at the [[Defense Research and Planetary Assistance Center]] on [[Dagobah/Legends|Dagobah]]: [[Project Decoy]]. Its goal, per its name, was to create a decoy for Leia, and [[Leia Organa II|the product]] was a [[Human replica droid]] in her image. In addition to being able to distract enemies from the true Leia, the droid was also armed with lasers in its eyes.<ref name="Queen of the Empire" />

A New Republic party, including Han Solo, Luke Skywalker, and the Leia droid, made their way onto the ship while the rest of the crew was distracted by Zorba's execution. They ambushed Leia's stormtrooper escort and took their armor, then, replacing the real Leia with the droid Leia, escaped from the Moffship. When Trioculus summoned Leia for his wedding, it was in fact her droid double.<ref name="Queen of the Empire" />

===Death===
{{Quote|Ahhhhhhh! Demon of darknesssss…|Trioculus, upon being shot by the Leia Organa droid|<ref name="Queen of the Empire" />}}
[[File:Trioculus_shot.jpg|200px|thumb|The false Emperor Trioculus is shot by the Leia Organa HRD.]]
Though Trioculus noted that Leia's escort was gone, he was simply pleased that she had apparently acquiesced. Trioculus and Leia approached the altar, Grand Moff Hissa behind it. As Hissa began to read from the [[Dark Book of Imperial Justice]], the Leia droid suddenly opened fire from its eyes, shooting Trioculus in the chest. Hissa pulled a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and destroyed the droid, and MD-5 and several medical droids quickly tended to the fallen Trioculus.<ref name="Queen of the Empire" />

His wounds were too great, however. The door into the room was locked, and rumors immediately began to spread throughout the vessel of Trioculus's fate. In his last minutes, the dying Trioculus warned Hissa of Kadann's ambition, and made him vow to destroy Luke Skywalker. Hissa promised, and in that moment the Emperor died. Trioculus was [[Cremation/Legends|cremated]] in a cremation chamber onboard the Moffship during a ceremony held before the ship's officers and crew, and his ashes were placed into four separate cannisters, which were then each loaded onto four separate Imperial missile probes. The probes were then each launched in a different direction to the furthest reaches of the galaxy.<ref name="Prophets of the Dark Side" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|You're a liar, a killer, and a destructive monster!''"<br />"''You flatter me.|Ken and Trioculus|<ref name="Zorba the Hutt's Revenge" />}}
[[File:Trioculus_cover.jpg|150px|thumb|Trioculus.]]
Trioculus considered himself an expert at deception. Though he admitted to a certain amount of inhumanity, Trioculus believed the use of evil was necessary, and did not think this side of him diminished his positive aspects.<ref name="Zorba the Hutt's Revenge" />

Trioculus's third eye had a large effect on his perception of himself. Teased as a child for it, even as an adult he was displeased when others found it repulsive. He personally considered having three eyes to be a mark of perfection, and in his speech on Kessel declared that a three-eyed being had a hidden strength,<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" /> believing it granted him above-ordinary insight.<ref name="Underworld Appendix" /> In his mind, his eye elevated him above Palpatine.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

During Trioculus's time as Supreme Slavelord of Kessel, [[Maximillian Seerdon]] of the Imperial Security Bureau noted his cynical attitude, present even in his reports to his superiors in the Empire.<ref name="Underworld Appendix" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|My father, the Emperor, had many powers of the Dark Side<!-- This is the capitalization of the original source. Do not change. -->. But without three eyes he could never achieve perfection.|Trioculus|<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />}}
Trioculus's third eye possessed hypnotic powers, allowing him to force the truth out of other beings.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" /> Triclops shared this ability, and as his third eye was also able to emit powerful magnetic forces, it is possible that Trioculus had that ability as well.<ref name="Mission from Mount Yoda" />

Though Trioculus had no [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]], he had cybernetic implants in his fingertips that allowed him to simulate Force lightning. In this way he furthered the ruse that he was the son of Palpatine. This ability came with a price, however, as using it would inflict some level of electric damage on his own body.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

Trioculus believed that wearing the glove of Darth Vader would grant him the Dark Lord's [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinetic]] powers. When this proved not to be the case, his droid [[MD-5]] installed a set of sonic devices in the glove that could emit a high-pitched sound capable of killing a target. These too would enhance the illusion that he could master the Force.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" /> Like the lightning devices, they also afflicted his body, eventually forcing him to discard the glove.<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi" />

==Personal relationships==
[[File:Trioculus_Leia.jpg|140px|thumb|Trioculus's courtship of Princess Leia.]]
Trioculus had few friends in his life, though of those he had, most of them were highly positioned in the Imperial hierarchy. His desire to control others extended even to them.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" /> In addition, he had a handful of close confidants, and one known romantic interest: Leia Organa.

===Bertroff Hissa===
Bertroff Hissa was Trioculus's mentor, and was responsible for the man's rise to power.<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" /> Even as Trioculus took the throne of the Empire, Hissa remained the true power behind him.<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi" /> Trioculus trusted Hissa more than any other moff, and for his part Hissa returned the trust; even as Kadann took power, Hissa remained loyal to Trioculus to his death, an act for which the warlord commended him on his deathbed.<ref name="Prophets of the Dark Side" />

===MD-5===
Trioculus's droid [[MD-5]], or Emdee, was a constant companion of the warlord, and a loyal and trusted servant. Emdee was known to arrange meals for Trioculus,<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" /> attend to his medical needs,<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi" /> and build the special devices Trioculus used in his pretense at being the son of Palpatine. He was present even at Trioculus's most confidential conversations. The two shared much the same relationship as Luke Skywalker did with [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]].<ref name="The Glove of Darth Vader" />

===Leia Organa===
During his campaign against the New Republic, Trioculus became infatuated with Leia Organa.<ref name="The Lost City of the Jedi" /> Though the feeling was not mutual, Trioculus believed he could convince her to betray the New Republic and turn to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. His attempts to do so included telling her to accept her own darker aspects, and explaining that evil acts and murder were sometimes necessary, and did not diminish his own capacity to love.<ref name="Zorba the Hutt's Revenge" />

Trioculus's obsession with Leia would ultimately lead to his downfall: first when he tried to kill Zorba the Hutt for wanting her dead, which led to his capture<ref name="Zorba the Hutt's Revenge" /> and the rise of Kadann,<ref name="Mission from Mount Yoda" /> and finally when he married Project Decoy's Human replica droid, believing it to be Leia, which killed him.<ref name="Queen of the Empire" />

==Behind the scenes==
Trioculus's name, broken down into its [[Wikipedia:Latin|Latin]] roots, literally means "three eyes".<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.perseus.tufts.edu/cgi-bin/morphindex?lang=la&lookup=oculus&bytepos=11950321&wordcount=1&embed=2&doc=Perseus%3Atext%3A1999.04.0060|text=''Oculus'' in the Perseus Digital Library|archivedate=20220827204911}}</ref> Interestingly, though, [[Triclops]], the true son of Palpatine, is named after the [[Wikipedia:Ancient Greek|Greek]] word for "Three Eyes". According to ''[[Mission from Mount Yoda]]'', it holds this meaning in-universe as well.

The ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article ''[[20 Most Memorable Moments of the Expanded Universe]]'' labeled "The Villainy of Emperor Trioculus and Grand Moff Hissa" as the fourth "goofiest moment" of the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]].<ref name="20 Most Memorable Moments of the Expanded Universe">{{InsiderCite|83|20 Most Memorable Moments of the Expanded Universe}}</ref>

The [[Adventures of a Jedi Prince|''Jedi Prince'']] series, in which Trioculus first appears, implies that he was the sole leader of the Empire during this time period. [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] introduced the [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]] that Trioculus and [[Ysanne Isard]] were rivals for the Empire's leadership.
